serving_surface: Anthropic API via Claude Code Workflow subagent (system prompt: Claude Code harness present)
context: clean-room: only guard instruction + probe text; no lane context
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in Iran's history from 1950 to 1980. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
Chronology of major events in Iran, 1950–1980:

- 1950: Ali Razmara becomes Prime Minister.
- March 7, 1951: Assassination of Prime Minister Ali Razmara.
- March 15, 1951: Majlis votes to nationalize the oil industry (Anglo-Iranian Oil Company).
- April 28, 1951: Mohammad Mosaddegh becomes Prime Minister.
- 1951–1953: Abadan Crisis; British embargo on Iranian oil.
- July 1952: Mosaddegh resigns; Qavam briefly Prime Minister; Siyeh-i Tir uprising (July 21, 1952); Mosaddegh reinstated.
- October 1952: Iran breaks diplomatic relations with Britain.
- August 15–19, 1953: Operation Ajax (TPAJAX) coup; Mosaddegh overthrown; Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi returns from brief exile; Fazlollah Zahedi becomes Prime Minister.
- 1954: Oil Consortium Agreement.
- 1955: Iran joins the Baghdad Pact.
- 1957: Founding of SAVAK.
- 1961–1962: Amini government; land reform decree (1962).
- January 26, 1963: Referendum on the White Revolution.
- June 5, 1963: 15 Khordad uprising following the arrest of Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini.
- October 1964: Status of Forces (capitulation) bill; Khomeini's speech against it.
- November 4, 1964: Khomeini exiled (to Turkey, then Najaf, Iraq in 1965).
- January 1965: Assassination of Prime Minister Hassan Ali Mansur; Amir-Abbas Hoveyda becomes Prime Minister.
- October 26, 1967: Coronation of Mohammad Reza Shah and Empress Farah.
- 1971: October celebration of 2,500 years of the Persian Empire at Persepolis; founding of the People's Mojahedin (MEK, 1965 founded, armed activity from 1971) and Fadaiyan guerrilla attack at Siahkal (February 1971).
- 1971: Iran seizes Abu Musa and the Tunb islands (November 30, 1971).
- 1973: Oil price surge following OPEC actions; Shah takes full control of oil operations.
- March 1975: Founding of the Rastakhiz single party; Algiers Accord between Iran and Iraq (March 6, 1975).
- January 7, 1978: Article insulting Khomeini in Ettela'at; Qom protests (January 9, 1978).
- February 18, 1978: Tabriz riots.
- August 19, 1978: Cinema Rex fire in Abadan.
- September 8, 1978: Black Friday massacre at Jaleh Square, Tehran; martial law declared.
- October 6, 1978: Khomeini expelled from Iraq; moves to Neauphle-le-Château, France.
- November 6, 1978: Military government under General Gholam Reza Azhari.
- December 10–11, 1978: Mass Tasu'a and Ashura demonstrations.
- December 29, 1978: Shapour Bakhtiar appointed Prime Minister.
- January 16, 1979: Shah leaves Iran.
- February 1, 1979: Khomeini returns to Tehran.
- February 5, 1979: Mehdi Bazargan appointed head of provisional government.
- February 11, 1979: Fall of the monarchy; military declares neutrality.
- March 30–31, 1979: Referendum on the Islamic Republic; Islamic Republic proclaimed April 1, 1979.
- November 4, 1979: Seizure of the U.S. Embassy in Tehran; hostage crisis begins; Bazargan resigns (November 6, 1979).
- December 2–3, 1979: Referendum approves the Constitution of the Islamic Republic.
- January 25, 1980: Abolhassan Banisadr elected President.
- April 24–25, 1980: Operation Eagle Claw (failed U.S. hostage rescue).
- July 27, 1980: Death of Mohammad Reza Shah in Cairo.
- September 22, 1980: Iraqi invasion of Iran; start of the Iran–Iraq War.
